title: galium rubrum var. alpicola
intro: Galium rubrum var. alpicola, also known as Alpine Bedstraw, is a variety of the Galium rubrum plant, which belongs to the Galium genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. Alpine Bedstraw has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving arthritis and joint pain, and treating kidney and urinary issues. Alpine Bedstraw can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it has been used in traditional herbal remedies for centuries.
